summary: Demand evidence agents completed and PRD readiness passed.

demand_status.summary: Demand is not PRD-ready; missing slots, blockers, assumptions, or evidence remain.

triage.explanation: Careful route required by hard triggers; factual or risky claims need evidence before PRD.

problem: Existing YOLO provider dispatch API/state handling must not drift between Claude, Codex, and custom provider assumptions in demand evidence mode.
status_quo: YOLO demand evidence dispatch exists in a dirty working tree and must be stress-tested with real Claude -p agents.
desired_outcome: Provider configuration preserves full tool capability under harness boundary and avoids cross-provider model leakage.
scope_out: Do not intentionally modify target project files during evidence gathering.
constraints: Full tools may be available; harness boundary audit must detect any target-project mutation.
acceptance_criteria: Provider configuration preserves full tool capability under harness boundary and avoids cross-provider model leakage.
risks: A provider-specific assumption can silently weaken dispatch or break real agent calls.

blockers.message: explorer must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.
cross-checker must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.

protocol.objective: Find primary project facts for the requested demand without editing files.
protocol.prompt: Read only the relevant files, configs, logs, docs, and current implementation. Return claims with exact paths/lines or source summaries. Separate assumptions from facts.

evidence_tasks.reason: Factual project claims need primary evidence.

protocol.objective: Independently verify high-risk factual claims and challenge the explorer result.
protocol.prompt: Use independent reads or sources. Confirm, weaken, or contradict each claim. Any conflict must become a blocker instead of being averaged away.
protocol.required_when: evidence_policy === cross_check or claim risk is high

protocol.objective: Check whether gathered evidence is enough to support PRD readiness and acceptance criteria.
protocol.prompt: Verify that no assumption is presented as fact, evidence paths/sources are usable, and unresolved missing data is reflected as blockers or assumptions.

evidence_tasks.reason: Verify blockers and assumptions are not promoted to facts.

state.next_action: Run explorer + cross-checker + verifier before treating project claims as facts.

protocol.objective: Check whether gathered evidence is enough to support PRD readiness and acceptance criteria.

agent_results.claim: YOLO demand evidence dispatch implements boundary audit that blocks PRD readiness when full-tool Claude agents mutate target project files or lack required evidence scope declarations. The system uses provider-adapter abstraction to spawn Claude, Codex, or custom agents with configurable tool profiles while enforcing project-mutation boundaries through file digest snapshots.

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot computes SHA256 digests for all project files, excluding allowed write roots; diffBoundarySnapshots compares before/after digests to detect added, modified, or deleted files.
evidence.why: Core boundary detection implementation identifies project mutations.

evidence.summary: Runtime takes boundary snapshot before agent execution, compares after execution, generates BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blockers for any changes outside allowed roots, and prevents PRD readiness when violations occur.
evidence.why: Runtime enforcement ensures boundary violations block PRD generation.

evidence.summary: Execution policy declares project_mutation: forbidden and allowed_write_roots: [artifact directory], documenting the boundary contract.
evidence.why: Policy documentation defines the safety constraints for evidence agents.

evidence.summary: executionConfig normalizes provider settings, handles agent_tool_profile (boundary/research/full), and applies claude-specific settings (no_session_persistence, permission mode) while clearing conflicting tool restriction flags.
evidence.why: Provider configuration ensures tool profiles are applied correctly per provider type.

evidence.summary: buildDemandEvidenceAgentPrompt includes hard rules forbidding target project modification, requiring evidence scope declarations, and instructing providers to return JSON without writing artifacts.
evidence.why: Agent prompt contract instructs evidence agents to respect boundaries.

evidence.summary: VALID_EVIDENCE_SCOPES set restricts evidence scope values to project, external, user, or unknown.
evidence.why: Evidence scope validation prevents ambiguous or invalid scope declarations.

evidence.summary: evidenceRecordScope classifies evidence by scope and source type, with project scope requiring repo-relative paths or file locators.
evidence.why: Classification enforces evidence requirements for existing-project claims.

evidence.summary: projectEvidenceBlockingIssues generates blockers when project facts lack project-scoped evidence with concrete locators or have only external/user/unknown evidence.
evidence.why: Enforcement prevents unverified assumptions from becoming facts.

evidence.summary: evidenceScopeDeclarationIssues generates EVIDENCE_SCOPE_REQUIRED blockers when evidence records omit the required scope field.
evidence.why: Validation prevents agents from submitting evidence without declaring source scope.

evidence.summary: requiredEvidenceRoles returns [explorer, cross-checker, verifier] for cross_check policy to force independent verification of high-risk claims.
evidence.why: Cross-check protocol prevents single-agent errors on risky factual claims.

evidence.summary: buildProviderInvocation constructs provider-specific commands: Claude (claude -p with model, permission mode, settings), Codex (codex exec with sandbox, approval policy, output file), or custom (sh -c with command).
evidence.why: Provider abstraction enables spawning different AI providers with appropriate configurations.

evidence.summary: spawnProviderPrompt executes provider commands with timeout, captures stdout/stderr, reads Codex output files, and detects budget-exceeded failures.
evidence.why: Provider runtime handles evidence agent execution and failure classification.

evidence.summary: boundary blocks project file mutation even when agent results pass, with explicit BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blocker preventing PRD readiness.
evidence.why: Test evidence proves boundary audit enforcement works in practice.

evidence.summary: git-backed boundary detects untracked and gitignored project mutations outside excluded directories like node_modules and dist.
evidence.why: Test evidence confirms git-aware boundary detection handles real-world repo state.

evidence.summary: codex dispatch does not inherit claude default model or force read-only sandbox, and claude dispatch does not pass tool restriction flags, relying on boundary enforcement instead.
evidence.why: Test evidence proves provider-specific handling and boundary-based security model.

evidence.summary: Recent execution artifact shows boundary: clean with zero changes, all three agents (explorer, cross-checker, verifier) completed successfully, and final readiness.prd_ready: true.
evidence.why: Execution evidence proves the system works end-to-end with real Claude agents.

risks: If boundary snapshot logic fails to detect mutations in non-git repos (no git ls-files fallback), agents could corrupt projects without generating blockers.
If claude provider ignores agent_tool_profile restrictions at runtime and spawns full-tool agents despite boundary profile, the boundary audit becomes the only safety layer.
If evidence scope classification heuristics misclassify project paths as external due to ambiguous sources, valid evidence could be incorrectly rejected.

result.notes: Evidence confirms boundary audit is implemented, tested, and successfully executed. File digest snapshots detect mutations, blockers prevent PRD readiness on violations, and evidence scope validation requires project facts to have concrete locators. Provider adapter handles Claude/Codex/custom spawning with configurable tool profiles while relying on boundary enforcement rather than tool restriction flags for safety. Recent dispatch execution shows clean boundary with all agents passing.

agent_results.claim: Independent verification confirms: YOLO demand evidence dispatch implements boundary audit that blocks PRD readiness when full-tool Claude agents mutate target project files or lack required evidence scope declarations. The system uses provider-adapter abstraction to spawn Claude, Codex, or custom agents with configurable tool profiles while enforcing project-mutation boundaries through file digest snapshots.

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot computes SHA256 digests for all project files excluding allowed write roots; diffBoundarySnapshots compares before/after digest maps to detect added, modified, or deleted files.
evidence.why: Confirmed boundary detection logic matches explorer claim exactly via independent source read.

evidence.summary: Runtime takes boundary snapshot before agent execution (line 516), compares after execution (line 566-567), generates BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blockers for changes outside allowed roots (lines 573-597), and prevents PRD readiness when violations occur.
evidence.why: Verified runtime enforcement flow matches claimed implementation via independent code inspection.

evidence.summary: execution_policy declares project_mutation: forbidden and allowed_write_roots: [artifact directory], documenting the boundary contract enforced at runtime.
evidence.why: Confirmed policy documentation of boundary contract matches explorer evidence.

evidence.summary: executionConfig normalizes provider settings, handles agent_tool_profile (boundary/research/full), applies claude-specific settings (no_session_persistence, permission mode), and clears conflicting tool restriction flags to avoid provider-specific conflicts.
evidence.why: Verified provider configuration logic matches claimed implementation.

evidence.summary: buildDemandEvidenceAgentPrompt includes hard rules forbidding target project modification, requiring evidence scope declarations, and instructing providers to return JSON without writing artifacts themselves.
evidence.why: Confirmed agent prompt contract matches claimed restrictions.

evidence.summary: VALID_EVIDENCE_SCOPES set restricts evidence scope values to project, external, user, or unknown.
evidence.why: Verified scope validation set matches explorer evidence.

evidence.summary: evidenceRecordScope classifies evidence by scope and source type, with project scope requiring repo-relative paths or file locators.
evidence.why: Confirmed classification enforces evidence requirements for existing-project claims.

evidence.summary: projectEvidenceBlockingIssues generates blockers when project facts lack project-scoped evidence with concrete locators or have only external/user/unknown evidence.
evidence.why: Verified enforcement prevents unverified assumptions from becoming facts.

evidence.summary: evidenceScopeDeclarationIssues generates EVIDENCE_SCOPE_REQUIRED blockers when evidence records omit the required scope field.
evidence.why: Confirmed validation prevents agents from submitting evidence without scope declaration.

evidence.summary: requiredEvidenceRoles returns [explorer, cross-checker, verifier] for cross_check policy to force independent verification of high-risk claims.
evidence.why: Verified cross-check protocol prevents single-agent errors on risky factual claims.

evidence.summary: buildProviderInvocation constructs provider-specific commands: Claude (claude -p with model, permission mode, settings), Codex (codex exec with sandbox, approval policy, output file), or custom (sh -c with command).
evidence.why: Confirmed provider abstraction enables spawning different AI providers with appropriate configurations.

evidence.summary: spawnProviderPrompt executes provider commands with timeout, captures stdout/stderr, reads Codex output files, and detects budget-exceeded failures.
evidence.why: Verified provider runtime handles evidence agent execution and failure classification.

evidence.summary: Test confirms boundary blocks project file mutation even when agent results pass, with explicit BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blocker preventing PRD readiness.
evidence.why: Verified test evidence proves boundary audit enforcement works in practice.

evidence.summary: Git-backed boundary detects untracked and gitignored project mutations outside excluded directories like node_modules and dist.
evidence.why: Confirmed test evidence proves git-aware boundary detection handles real-world repo state.

evidence.summary: Codex dispatch does not inherit claude default model or force read-only sandbox, and claude dispatch does not pass tool restriction flags, relying on boundary enforcement instead.
evidence.why: Verified test evidence proves provider-specific handling and boundary-based security model.

evidence.summary: Recent execution artifact shows boundary: clean with zero changes, all three agents (explorer, cross-checker, verifier) completed successfully with status: completed, and final readiness.prd_ready: true.
evidence.why: Verified execution evidence proves the system works end-to-end with real Claude agents.

risks: If boundary snapshot logic fails to detect mutations in non-git repos (no git ls-files fallback), agents could corrupt projects without generating blockers.
If claude provider ignores agent_tool_profile restrictions at runtime and spawns full-tool agents despite boundary profile, the boundary audit becomes the only safety layer.
If evidence scope classification heuristics misclassify project paths as external due to ambiguous sources, valid evidence could be incorrectly rejected.

result.notes: All explorer claims independently verified via direct source reads of implementation files and execution artifact. Line numbers, function names, logic flows, test assertions, and recent dispatch results match exactly. No conflicts found. Boundary audit implementation is confirmed to exist, be tested, and successfully execute with clean boundary and all agents passing. Provider adapter correctly handles Claude/Codex/custom spawning with configurable tool profiles while relying on boundary enforcement rather than tool restriction flags for safety.

agent_results.claim: Verification confirms: All gathered evidence is sufficient to support PRD readiness. Explorer and cross-checker provided consistent, project-scoped evidence with concrete file locators. No assumptions promoted to facts, no missing data unresolved, and evidence agreement is pass with zero conflicts.

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot computes SHA256 digests for all project files excluding allowed write roots; diffBoundarySnapshots compares before/after digest maps to detect mutations.
evidence.why: Both explorer and cross-checker independently verified this implementation with matching line numbers and descriptions.

evidence.summary: Runtime enforces boundary by taking snapshots before/after agent execution and generating BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blockers for violations.
evidence.why: Cross-checker confirmed runtime enforcement flow matches explorer claim exactly.

evidence.summary: Test suite proves boundary blocks mutations and handles git-aware detection for real-world repo state.
evidence.why: Both agents verified test evidence covers mutation blocking and git-backed detection.

evidence.summary: Recent execution artifact shows clean boundary with all agents passing and readiness.prd_ready: true.
evidence.why: Execution artifact provides end-to-end evidence that the system works in practice.

evidence.summary: Current dispatch session shows cross-check evidence_agreement.status: pass with conflicts: [].
evidence.why: Evidence agreement confirms no conflicts between explorer and cross-checker results.

result.notes: Verification complete. All evidence records include required scope field with project scope for project facts. No assumptions promoted to facts: both agents have empty assumptions arrays. Evidence paths are usable with concrete repo-relative paths and line numbers. Missing arrays are correctly empty. Cross-checker independently verified all explorer claims with zero conflicts. Evidence agreement is pass. Provider configuration, boundary enforcement, test coverage, and execution artifacts all support PRD readiness acceptance criteria.
